extraction.one_line_summary: PETERSEN Mads won a breakaway sprint on transition stage 13 after rivals made tactical errors pulling for him with 10km to go
tactical_patterns: PETERSEN successfully hid in breakaway final 10km while HOULE and WRIGHT burned matches pulling, revealing tactical naivety
SAGAN Peter's early breakaway attempt rejected by peloton due to sprint handicapping—faster riders avoided cooperating with him
GC teams completely neutralized transition stage despite 7000ft climbing, protecting leaders after Alpe d'Huez damage assessment

rider_signals.PETERSEN Mads: positive: forced rivals to leadout in final kilometer showing superior positioning instincts and confidence managing faster raw speed
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: peloton actively refused cooperation when he attacked, suggesting reputation makes him unmarketable breakaway partner
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: team successfully protected 2:22 gap third consecutive day suggesting UAE lacks firepower for transition-stage attacks
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: negative: dropped immediately on cat-3 climb despite making breakaway, exposed lack of form after mountain stages
